caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the concept of Figure of Merit (FOM) as part of Tutorial 3. The presentation is given by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay. The slide shows a thermodynamic diagram with various processes labeled, such as constant temperature (T=const), constant pressure (p=const), and constant enthalpy (h=const). Initially, the FOM is calculated as -Wc/mf = 2772.1. As the video progresses, another calculation is shown, updating the FOM to -Wc/mf = 476.0. This suggests a step-by-step analysis or comparison of different scenarios or systems within the context of cryogenic engineering, likely aimed at evaluating their efficiency or performance. The visual elements and calculations indicate a detailed explanation of thermodynamic principles applied to cryogenic processes.
